events.label: reasoning
events.text:
We are asked to evaluate a panelboard installation & makeup at rough-complete stage in an industrial site. The scenario says: "Closeout photos for Panelboard installation & makeup show the installed work following the referenced detail. Clearances, labels, supports, and terminations shown for this element are in place, and the inspection card has no open correction note. Reference material on hand: NEC Art. 408, 110.26 (working clearance), 200.4." So it seems that the work is in order: photos show it's installed per detail, clearances, labels, supports, terminations are in place, inspection card has no open correction note. So it's compliant. The stage is rough-complete. No component conditions are flagged. I need to output JSON with decision: pass, risk: low? The work is not dangerous, so risk low. Work stage: rough-complete. Component conditions: empty list. Percent complete: maybe 100%? But rough-complete means roughed in, not yet closed out. So maybe 90%? I'll put 95%. Value: no numeric reading, so 0. Confidence: high, 0.95. Other fields: event_time, rate, sound_source, order, workable are not relevant, so set to default. Findings: maybe "All clearances, labels, supports, and terminations are in place and no open correction notes." Actions: none. Rationale: "The installed panelboard appears to comply with NEC Art. 408, 110.26, and 200.4, as evidenced by the closeout photos and inspection card." References: ["NEC Art. 408", "NEC 110.26", "NEC 200.4"].

I'll output JSON with decision "pass", risk "low", work_stage "rough-complete", component_conditions: [], percent_complete: 95, value: 0, confidence: 0.95, event_time: 0, rate: 0, order: [], workable: [], findings: ["All components installed per referenced detail, clearances, labels, supports, and terminations are in place"], actions: ["No corrective actions needed"], rationale: "The field condition matches the engineered detail, inspection card is clear, and applicable NEC articles are satisfied.", references: ["NEC Art. 408", "NEC 110.26", "NEC 200.4"].

I'll write to /app/answer.json.

events.label: response
events.text:
{
  "decision": "pass",
  "risk": "low",
  "work_stage": "rough-complete",
  "component_conditions": [],
  "percent_complete": 95,
  "value": 0,
  "sound_source": "",
  "confidence": 0.95,
  "event_time": 0,
  "rate": 0,
  "order": [],
  "workable": [],
  "findings": ["All components installed per referenced detail, clearances, labels, supports, and terminations are in place"],
  "actions": ["No corrective actions needed"],
  "rationale": "The field condition matches the engineered detail, inspection card is clear, and applicable NEC articles are satisfied.",
  "references": ["NEC Art. 408", "NEC 110.26", "NEC 200.4"]
}
